name: Rutherford B. Hayes
portraitCredit: Portrait via Wikimedia Commons. Public domain.

bio.scholar: Rutherford B. Hayes, the nineteenth President, took office after one of the most disputed elections in American history. Known as the 'Dark-Horse President,' Hayes won the 1876 election only after a special electoral commission awarded him contested electoral votes by a single vote margin. He ended Reconstruction by withdrawing federal troops from the South and pursued civil service reform. Hayes earned a reputation for personal integrity, declaring his desire that 'sectionalism as a factor in our politics should disappear.'
bio.explorer: Rutherford B. Hayes became the nineteenth president after the most confusing election ever! The vote was so close and messy that a special group had to decide who won. Hayes wanted to heal the country and stop the fighting between the North and South. He was known for being very honest.

text.scholar: Ended Reconstruction by withdrawing federal troops from the South, a decision that had lasting consequences for civil rights.
text.explorer: He pulled soldiers out of the South, ending the period called Reconstruction. This had big effects on the rights of Black Americans.

sources.citation: Miller Center: rutherford b hayes - Key Events

text.scholar: Pursued civil service reform to reduce corruption and political patronage in government hiring.
text.explorer: He worked to make government hiring more fair — based on skill, not who you knew.

sources.citation: Miller Center: rutherford b hayes - Key Events

text.scholar: Earned a reputation for integrity as both a soldier and statesman, working to reduce sectionalism in American politics.
text.explorer: People respected him for being honest and trying to bring the North and South closer together.

sources.citation: Miller Center: rutherford b hayes - Key Events

text.scholar: Hayes won the presidency after one of the most disputed and controversial elections in American history, decided by a special electoral commission.
text.explorer: He won the most confusing election ever — a special group had to be created just to figure out who won!

sources.citation: Miller Center: rutherford b hayes - Key Events

text.scholar: He was nicknamed the 'Dark-Horse President' because he was not expected to win his party's nomination.
text.explorer: He was called the 'Dark-Horse President' because nobody expected him to win.

sources.citation: Miller Center: rutherford b hayes - Key Events

text.scholar: He and his wife Lucy had eight children and were known for banning alcohol from the White House, earning Lucy the nickname 'Lemonade Lucy.'
text.explorer: His wife Lucy banned alcohol from the White House, so people jokingly called her 'Lemonade Lucy!'
